Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Preparing for a .NET interview can be overwhelming, especially when there are hundreds of concepts to learn. In this series, I’ll cover the Top 30 .NET interview questions that are commonly asked for 2+ years of experience.
Each question includes:

• A simple explanation

• An interview-ready answer

• A practical example
Let’s begin with the first 10 must-know .NET interview questions.
1. What is Dependency Injection (DI)?
Answer
Dependency Injection (DI) is a design pattern where a class receives its required objects (dependencies) from the outside instead of creating them itself.
This helps make the application:

• Loosely coupled

• Easier to maintain

• Easier to test
Example
Without Dependency Injection:
public class EmployeeService
{
private EmployeeRepository repository = new EmployeeRepository();
}With Dependency Injection:
public class EmployeeService
{
private readonly IEmployeeRepository repository;
public EmployeeService(IEmployeeRepository repository)
{
this.repository = repository;
}
}Interview Tip: ASP.NET Core has built-in Dependency Injection using its IoC (Inversion of Control) container.
2. Explain Service Lifetimes: Singleton, Scoped, and Transient.
Answer
ASP.NET Core provides three service lifetimes.
Singleton
Only one instance is created and shared throughout the application’s lifetime.
Example: Logging service.
Scoped
One instance is created per HTTP request.
Example: DbContext
Transient
A new instance is created every time the service is requested.
Example: Email service.
Registration Example
builder.Services.AddSingleton<IEmailService, EmailService>();
builder.Services.AddScoped<IEmployeeService, EmployeeService>();
builder.Services.AddTransient<ILogService, LogService>();3. What is Middleware in ASP.NET Core?
Answer
Middleware is software that handles HTTP requests and responses in the ASP.NET Core request pipeline.
Every request passes through middleware before reaching the controller.
Common middleware includes:

• Authentication

• Authorization

• Exception Handling

• CORS

• Routing
Example
app.UseAuthentication();
app.UseAuthorization();
app.MapControllers();Interview Tip: Middleware executes in the order it is registered.
4. What is the difference between Authentication and Authorization?
Authentication
Authentication answers:
“Who are you?”
It verifies the user’s identity.
Example: Logging in with a username and password.
Authorization
Authorization answers:
“What are you allowed to do?”
It determines what resources the authenticated user can access.
Example: Only an Admin can delete users.
5. Explain async and await.
Answer
async and await are keywords used to perform asynchronous operations without blocking the application's main thread.
They improve application performance and responsiveness.
Example
public async Task<List<Employee>> GetEmployees()
{
return await _context.Employees.ToListAsync();
}Benefits

• Better scalability

• Faster response time

• Non-blocking operations
6. What is the difference between IEnumerable and IQueryable?
IEnumerable

• Executes the query in memory.

• Suitable for working with in-memory collections.
IQueryable

• Executes the query in the database.

• Fetches only the required data.
Example
IQueryable<Employee> employees =
_context.Employees.Where(e => e.Department == "HR");Interview Tip: Use IQueryable when working with Entity Framework because filtering happens in the database, improving performance.
7. What is Entity Framework Core?
Answer
Entity Framework Core (EF Core) is Microsoft’s Object-Relational Mapper (ORM).
It allows developers to interact with databases using C# objects instead of writing SQL queries manually.
Example
var employees = _context.Employees.ToList();Instead of writing:
SELECT * FROM Employees;Entity Framework Core automatically generates the SQL query.
8. Explain REST API and HTTP Methods.
Answer
REST (Representational State Transfer) is an architectural style used to build web APIs.
The most common HTTP methods are:
MethodPurposeGETRetrieve dataPOSTCreate new dataPUTUpdate an entire resourcePATCHUpdate part of a resourceDELETEDelete data
Example
GET /api/employees
POST /api/employees
PUT /api/employees/1
PATCH /api/employees/1
DELETE /api/employees/19. What is the difference between First() and FirstOrDefault()?
First()

• Returns the first matching record.

• Throws an exception if no record is found.
FirstOrDefault()

• Returns the first matching record.

• Returns null (or the default value) if no record is found.
Example
var employee =
_context.Employees.FirstOrDefault(e => e.Id == 1);Interview Tip: Use FirstOrDefault() when a record might not exist to avoid exceptions.
10. What is the difference between an Interface and an Abstract Class?
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Interface Example
public interface INotification
{
void Send();
}Abstract Class Example
public abstract class Vehicle
{
public void Start()
{
Console.WriteLine("Vehicle Started");
} public abstract void Drive();
}
Interview Tip: Use an interface when you want to define a contract. Use an abstract class when multiple classes share common behavior.
Conclusion
These are the first 10 .NET interview questions that every developer with 2+ years of experience should know. These concepts are commonly asked in interviews because they test your understanding of ASP.NET Core fundamentals, design patterns, and best practices.
If you found this article helpful, feel free to follow me for the next part in this interview preparation series.
